entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An important rainfed pulse crop grown in the rainy season that is subject to waterlogging.

entities.name: WATERLOGGING
entities.description: A condition of soil saturation that affects crop growth and survival.

entities.name: WATERLOGGING TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of pigeonpea genotypes to withstand and recover from waterlogging.

entities.name: MINI CORE PIGEONPEA GERMPLASM
entities.description: A collection of 146 pigeonpea genotypes screened for waterlogging tolerance.

entities.name: MORTALITY RATE
entities.description: The rate of plant death during waterlogging trials, influenced by environmental factors.

entities.name: VAPOUR PRESSURE DEFICITS
entities.description: Atmospheric conditions that influenced the mortality rate during waterlogging.

entities.name: RECOVERY PERIODS
entities.description: The time intervals following waterlogging during which plant recovery is observed.

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation in waterlogging response attributable to genetic factors.

entities.name: SURVIVAL COUNTS
entities.description: The number of surviving plants used to group genotypes based on tolerance.

entities.name: HIERARCHICAL CLUSTER ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to group survival counts into five distinctive tolerance groups.

entities.name: RAPID RECOVERY
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to recover quickly after a waterlogging event.

entities.name: ICP 7035
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype reported to be sensitive to waterlogging.

entities.name: ICP 8338
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype reported to be sensitive to waterlogging.

entities.name: ICP 13562
entities.description: A pigeonpea genotype reported to be sensitive to waterlogging.
